chunks.title: Frozen Versus Fresh Berries
chunks.summary: Frozen strawberries are highlighted for their affordability and nutritional preservation. The narrator explains the importance of mixing frozen and fresh fruits in smoothies, particularly due to the high pesticide levels in non-organic strawberries.
chunks.transcript: All right, next thing. Four pounds of organic strawberries. These are $3.20 a pound. So, I can get 4 lbs for $12.79. This is amazing. Why do I like frozen berries? Cheaper. Frozen berries are often picked at optimal ripeness and then it kind of locks in a lot of the nutrients. The one thing you lose with frozen berries is vitamin C. So, a lot of times in my smoothies, I'll do frozen blueberries, but I also try and get fresh blueberries. So, some mix. I don't want to use fresh blueberries for all of my smoothies because I have a lot of blueberries in the smoothie. It's just more cost effective and I'm getting a lot of nutrients with frozen. So, I'll use frozen strawberries and smoothies, but then I try and mix with some fresh stuff. Today, what did I have to eat? I had fresh orange juice. I got plenty of vitamin C for that. But I'm going to get these frozen strawberries. Super affordable. And as good as maybe even better than fresh berries. So some mix of like frozen and fresh fruit is not the worst idea. Why do I care about organic? Strawberries are always at the top of EWG's Dirty Dozen. These are some of the highest pesticide sprayed fruits out there. So I'm not going to eat any berries, whether it's cherries, blueberries, or strawberries, unless they're organic. Super important to me.

chunks.title: Egg Options at Costco
